source: Candidate legal-authority verification pack for deterministic citation, quote, and pin-cite checks. This is a research plan/domain vocabulary, not legal advice.
purpose: Constrain legal verification compiles to authority-ledger anatomy: citations, resolution status, metadata checks, quote checks, pin-cite locality, and explicit abstention on proposition-support questions.

lenses.purpose: Quote claims, quote-span matches, and pin-cite locality checks.
Proposition-support boundary rows that require human review instead of deterministic legal-support claims.

predicates.notes: Preserves one citation-like mention from a legal filing with source coordinate. citation_text is a normalized compact citation atom such as cite_576_us_644, not a proposition or source sentence.
Records deterministic citation lookup status against a declared authority inventory or external legal authority database. It does not decide good-law status or proposition support.

Records whether extracted metadata such as case name, court, year, reporter, volume, or first page matches the resolved authority metadata. Details stay in the report; the fact row carries only compact check status.
Records compact provenance for authority text used by quote and pin-cite checks. text_digest is a digest of normalized authority text or no_digest; authority prose stays out of typed atoms.
Records that the filing made a quoted-language claim near a citation. quote_digest is a digest, not the quoted prose. quote_location is a compact source coordinate.
Records whether the quote digest/text from the filing was found in the resolved authority text. matched_location is a page, paragraph, span id, no_match, or authority_unavailable.
Records whether a cited pin page or paragraph contains the quoted material when authority text has page/paragraph structure.

Records an explicit non-verification boundary, such as unresolved authority, unavailable lookup, short-form citation requiring context, unavailable authority text, unavailable pin cite, unsupported semantic legal proposition, or good-law status outside scope.
Tier 2 review target for a proposition attached to a citation. proposition_digest is compact; the proposition prose stays out of typed atoms.
Tier 2 candidate-span record, or explicit no_deterministic_span. This is not a support verdict.
Tier 2 support-assessment state. reviewed_support/reviewed_no_support require independent review or a future registered deterministic rule.
Records that a citation was used for a proposition and whether deterministic verification abstains or requires review. This row must not claim the proposition is legally correct.
Records accountable omissions for expected legal-authority verification carrier families. It is not an answer substitute.
